overrides.expect: A _ {i j} = \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} 1 & \text {if (i,j)\in E , i.e., there is an edge from vertex i to vertex j} \\ 0 & \text {otherwise.} \end{array} \right.
overrides.new: A _ {i j} = \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} 1 & \text {if }(i,j)\in E ,\ \text {i.e., there is an edge from vertex i to vertex j} \\ 0 & \text {otherwise.} \end{array} \right.

overrides.expect: d (s, t) = \min \left(d (s ^ {\prime}, t) + 1, d (s, t ^ {\prime}) + 1, \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} d (s ^ {\prime}, t ^ {\prime}) & \text {if s_{1} = t_{1}} \\ d (s ^ {\prime}, t ^ {\prime}) + 1 & \text {if s_{1} \neq t_{1}} \end{array} \right.\right).
overrides.new: d (s, t) = \min \left(d (s ^ {\prime}, t) + 1, d (s, t ^ {\prime}) + 1, \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} d (s ^ {\prime}, t ^ {\prime}) & \text {if }s_{1} = t_{1} \\ d (s ^ {\prime}, t ^ {\prime}) + 1 & \text {if }s_{1} \neq t_{1} \end{array} \right.\right).

overrides.expect: \begin{array}{l} \hline \text {COSINE INTEGRATION} \\ \text {Input: A list of integers x_{1},\ldots,x_{n}} \\ \text {Question: Is \int_{- \pi} ^{\pi} (\cos x_{1}\theta)(\cos x_{2}\theta)\cdots(\cos x_{n}\theta) d\theta\neq 0?} \\ \end{array}
overrides.new: \begin{array}{l} \hline \text {COSINE INTEGRATION} \\ \text {Input: A list of integers }x_{1},\ldots,x_{n} \\ \text {Question: Is }\int_{- \pi} ^{\pi} (\cos x_{1}\theta)(\cos x_{2}\theta)\cdots(\cos x_{n}\theta) d\theta\neq 0\text {?} \\ \end{array}

overrides.expect: \operatorname{Halts} (\Pi , x) = \left\{ \begin{array}{l l} \text {true} & \text {if \Pi halts on input x} \\ \text {false} & \text {otherwise} \end{array} \right.

\begin{array}{l l} & h (\mathbf {x}, y) \\ & \text {begin} \\ h (\mathbf {x}, y) & z := f (\mathbf {x}); \\ \text {begin} & \text {for y^{\prime} = 0 to y - 1 do} \\ \text {if y = 0 then return f(x);} & z := g (\mathbf {x}, y ^ {\prime}, z); \\ \text {else return g(x,y,h(x,y - 1));} & \text {end} \\ \text {end} & \text {return z}; \\ & \text {end} \end{array}
overrides.new: \begin{array}{l l} & h (\mathbf {x}, y) \\ & \text {begin} \\ h (\mathbf {x}, y) & z := f (\mathbf {x}); \\ \text {begin} & \text {for }y^{\prime} = 0\text { to } y - 1\text { do} \\ \text {if }y = 0\text { then return }f(x); & z := g (\mathbf {x}, y ^ {\prime}, z); \\ \text {else return }g(x,y,h(x,y - 1)); & \text {end} \\ \text {end} & \text {return }z; \\ & \text {end} \end{array}
